frameworks.name: probabilistic inference
frameworks.one_line: Models history-dependent bias as inference from prior trial observations

frameworks.name: recurrent attractor network with short-term facilitation
frameworks.one_line: Synaptic facilitation reshapes connectivity to encode trial history

frameworks.name: static-connectivity recurrent network
frameworks.one_line: Baseline working-memory network without short-term facilitation

core_question: How do synaptic facilitation dynamics produce serial-dependence bias in visual working memory across trials?
key_assumptions: Recalled target location can be biased by previous-trial target
Short-term facilitation can imprint trial history on network connectivity
Timescale separation justifies a low-dimensional bias description
manipulations: Compared network with short-term facilitation vs static connectivity
Varied target sequence statistics (repetitive vs uncorrelated sequences)
Linked probabilistic inference model to recurrent network computations
key_findings: Recalled target location is biased toward the previous-trial target
Facilitation-based network retains repetitively presented targets better than uncorrelated sequences
Low-dimensional description of trial-to-trial bias derived via timescale separation
interpretations: Short-term facilitation implements a prediction from prior observations, generating history-dependent bias
Interference is functionally adaptive when target sequences are correlated
